questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 58° and angle B measures 67°. What is the measure of angle C?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. Therefore, angle C = 180° − 58° − 67° = 55°.

questions.question: A right triangle has one acute angle measuring 34°. What is the measure of the other acute angle?
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, the two acute angles are complementary (sum to 90°). The other angle = 90° − 34° = 56°.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, angle P = 72°. If angle Q is twice angle R, what is the measure of angle Q?
questions.explanation: Let angle R = x. Then angle Q = 2x. So 72° + 2x + x = 180°, giving 3x = 108°, thus x = 36°. Angle Q = 2(36°) = 72°.

questions.question: Two sides of an isosceles triangle measure 8 cm and 8 cm. If the angle between them is 64°, what is the measure of each base angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the base angles (opposite the equal sides) are equal. Let each base angle = x. Then 64° + x + x = 180°, so 2x = 116°, and x = 58°.

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle measures 115°. If one of the non-adjacent interior angles measures 43°, what is the measure of the other non-adjacent interior angle?
questions.explanation: An exterior angle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles. So 115° = 43° + (other angle), giving the other angle = 72°.

questions.question: In a right triangle, one acute angle is 3 times the other acute angle. Find the measure of the smaller acute angle.
questions.explanation: Let the smaller acute angle = x. Then the larger = 3x. Since they're complementary: x + 3x = 90°, so 4x = 90°, and x = 22.5°.
